meta.title: StarCraft II Ground-Truth Timings & Costs Database

field_glossary.supply: supply cost (Zerglings 0.5 each; supply providers have supply 0 and provides_supply>0)
field_glossary.bt_faster: build/train/morph time in FASTER real-time seconds (what the in-game clock shows; use this for build-order math)
field_glossary.bt_normal: build time in NORMAL game-seconds (raw data value) = bt_faster * 1.399902
field_glossary.rt_faster: research time in FASTER real-time seconds
field_glossary.req: tech prerequisites that must exist first
field_glossary.morph_from: unit/building this is morphed from (source is consumed or transformed)
field_glossary.provides_supply: supply this structure/unit adds to your cap
field_glossary.at: structure the upgrade is researched at

time_convention.rule: Build/research times are stored internally in NORMAL game-seconds. The game DISPLAYS real-time seconds; on 'Faster' (ladder/tournament standard) the in-game clock ALSO runs in real-time. So faster_s = normal_s / 1.399902343750 (~/1.4). Build-order timestamps (mm:ss) and all in-game readouts are FASTER seconds. Primary column for planning = *_faster.
time_convention.liquipedia_caveat: Liquipedia is INTERNALLY INCONSISTENT: the Unit Statistics page and upgrade pages list FASTER values; the Building Statistics page lists NORMAL values. All values below are normalized so bt_faster/rt_faster is always the real-time (displayed) number.
time_convention.building_fix_2026_07_05: Building bt values were double-converted (real-time values had been labeled normal and divided again). Fixed: bt_faster now holds the real-time/Faster value, bt_normal recomputed as faster*1.399902. Empirically verified against a 5.0.16 EU ladder replay (2026-07-04): observed Pylon 17.9f, Gateway 46.4f, Cybernetics Core 35.7f, Stargate 42.9f, Nexus 71.4f, Assimilator 21.4f, Shield Battery 28.6f, Forge 32.1f, Fleet Beacon 42.9f, Robotics Facility 46.4f — all matching the corrected bt_faster. Unit times were already correct (observed Probe 12f, Stalker 27f, Oracle 37f). bt_normal snapped to the nearest multiple of 5 when within 1 (canonical Normal times are 5-multiples: CC 100, Barracks 65, Depot 30).

provenance_notes: Unit costs/supply/times: Liquipedia Unit Statistics (Faster), reconciled with 5.0.16 patch notes (Ghost supply 3, Ghost HP 100).
Building costs/times: Liquipedia Building Statistics (Normal, converted to Faster), with 5.0.16 fixes (Hatchery cost 300, CC/Nexus provides_supply 13, Hatchery/Lair/Hive provides_supply 4).
Upgrades: Liquipedia upgrade/building pages (Faster). Zerg Ground Carapace costs updated to 5.0.16 (100/150/200) — Liquipedia page still stale at 150/200/250.
Weapon/armor upgrade bt_normal values are back-computed (faster*1.4) and may differ +/-1s from the exact catalog integer.
Combat stats (hp/shield/armor/attributes) are supplementary and stable across recent patches; for exhaustive combat data (DPS, range, bonus-vs-type) consult Liquipedia unit pages.
Raw multi-agent research dump kept at scratchpad tasks/wxr9np23v.output for audit.
verify_flags: 5.0.16 is a very large patch; per-unit combat reworks beyond the captured cost/supply/time deltas (e.g. Ghost attack/Steady Targeting, Infestor Microbial Shroud rework, Mutalisk Glaives) are noted but combat numbers should be re-checked if used for fine balance.
Worker mining rates are distance/saturation-dependent approximations, not exact engine constants.

game_speed.clock: On Faster the in-game match clock runs in real-time, so clock seconds ARE faster-seconds: 1:00 on the clock = 60 faster-s. bt_faster values therefore align directly with build-order mm:ss.

start.note: 5.0.16 reduced starting workers 12->8. Zerg cap 12 = Hatchery(4) + starting Overlord(8). Terran/Protoss cap 13 = Command Center/Nexus(13).

resources_5_0_16.note: 5.0.16 economy rework: large patch 1800->1600, small patch 900->1100, geyser 2250->2500, rich vespene return 8->6. Intent: keep 1-3 base play viable longer.

mining.third_worker_per_patch_marginal: roughly half of the first two (~a saturated 3-worker patch ~2.5x a 1-worker patch)
mining.note: Rates are FASTER real-time approximations; exact income depends on patch distance and pathing. 5.0.16 changed patch SIZES (depletion), not per-trip yield.

ChronoBoost.note: Continuously boosted item finishes in 2/3 its normal time.

base_larva_spawn.note: 5.0.16 sped periodic larva 10.7->9.5 faster-s; natural spawn stops at 3 larvae, inject can exceed.

Terran.name: Reaper
Terran.note: Cliff jump; KD8 grenade; HP regen.

Terran.name: Ghost
Terran.note: 5.0.16: supply 2->3, HP 125->100, attack->15, range 6->7, Steady Targeting dmg->170 / energy 75.

Terran.name: Hellbat
Terran.note: Free ~4s transform from Hellion once Armory exists.

Protoss.name: Probe
Protoss.note: Worker; warps in structures then is free.

Protoss.name: Zealot
Protoss.note: Warp-in cd 22s (5.0.16). Charge upgrade at Twilight.

Protoss.name: High Templar
Protoss.note: Psi Storm (needs research), Feedback. Merges to Archon.

Protoss.name: Colossus
Protoss.note: Attacked by both air & ground weapons.

Protoss.name: Mothership
Protoss.note: 5.0.14 cost 300/300->400/400, supply 6->8. One per player. Cloak field, Time Warp.

Zerg.name: Drone
Zerg.note: Worker; consumed when morphing a structure (refunds its supply).

Zerg.name: Overlord
Zerg.note: Supply provider; slow. Speed via Pneumatized Carapace.

Zerg.name: Queen
Zerg.note: 5.0.14 cost 150->175. Spawn Larva, Transfusion, Creep Tumor. NOT from larva.

Zerg.name: Zergling
Zerg.note: 1 larva -> 2 Zerglings (50 min / larva). Speed via Metabolic Boost.

Zerg.name: Baneling
Zerg.note: Suicide AoE. +cost is on top of the Zergling.

Zerg.name: Roach
Zerg.note: Burrow-move with Tunneling Claws.

Zerg.name: Ravager
Zerg.note: Corrosive Bile. +cost/+supply on top of the Roach.

Zerg.name: Lurker
Zerg.note: Burrowed AoE line attack. Total supply 3 (2 hydra + 1).

Zerg.name: Infestor
Zerg.note: Fungal Growth, Neural Parasite (research), Microbial Shroud (5.0.16: no upgrade needed, range 12, gains auto-attack).

Zerg.name: Swarm Host
Zerg.note: Spawns free Locusts.

Zerg.name: Mutalisk
Zerg.note: Bouncing Glaive attack (5.0.16 prioritizes targets, arc 180).

Zerg.name: Corruptor
Zerg.note: Anti-air; Caustic Spray. Morphs to Brood Lord.

Zerg.name: Brood Lord
Zerg.note: Spawns Broodlings. +cost/+supply on top of the Corruptor.

Zerg.name: Viper
Zerg.note: Abduct, Blinding Cloud, Parasitic Bomb (5.0.16 Abduct hits sieged Tanks).

Zerg.name: Overseer
Zerg.note: Detector; Changeling, Oversight.

Terran.name: Command Center
Terran.note: 5.0.16 supply 15->13. Morphs to Orbital/Planetary; can lift.

Terran.name: Orbital Command
Terran.note: +cost on top of CC. Calldown MULE/Scan/Supply. Starts 50 energy.

Terran.name: Planetary Fortress
Terran.note: +cost on top of CC. Cannon; cannot lift; armor 3.

Terran.name: Barracks
Terran.note: Add-on: Reactor or Tech Lab. Can lift.

Terran.name: Tech Lab (add-on)
Terran.note: Unlocks advanced units & upgrades for that structure.

Protoss.name: Nexus
Protoss.note: 5.0.16 supply 15->13. Chrono Boost (starts 50 energy). Builds Probes & Mothership.

Protoss.name: Gateway
Protoss.note: Morphs to Warp Gate after research.

Protoss.name: Warp Gate
Protoss.note: 5.0.16: transform now costs 25/25 (was free). Warps units in (4s warp-in) and produces Gateway units ~40% faster.

Protoss.name: Cybernetics Core
Protoss.note: Unlocks Stalker/Sentry/Adept, air upgrades, Warp Gate research.

Protoss.name: Forge
Protoss.note: Ground weapon/armor/shield upgrades; enables Photon Cannon.

Protoss.name: Shield Battery
Protoss.note: 5.0.16 starts with 75 energy. Restores shields to nearby units.

Protoss.name: Twilight Council
Protoss.note: Charge, Blink, Resonating Glaives; enables Templar Archives/Dark Shrine.

Protoss.name: Robotics Bay
Protoss.note: Colossus, Disruptor; Thermal Lance, gravitic upgrades.

Protoss.name: Stargate
Protoss.note: Phoenix, Void Ray, Oracle, Tempest, Carrier.

Zerg.name: Hatchery
Zerg.note: 5.0.16 cost 275->300, supply 6->4. Spawns larva; morphs to Lair.

Zerg.name: Lair
Zerg.note: +cost on top of Hatchery. Unlocks tier-2 tech.

Zerg.name: Hive
Zerg.note: +cost on top of Lair. Unlocks tier-3 tech.

Zerg.name: Spawning Pool
Zerg.note: Zerglings, Queen; ling upgrades.

Zerg.name: Evolution Chamber
Zerg.note: Ground melee/missile/carapace upgrades; enables Spine/Spore.

Zerg.name: Spine Crawler
Zerg.note: Anti-ground static defense; uproots/moves on creep.

Zerg.name: Spore Crawler
Zerg.note: Detector; anti-air. 5.0.16 +5 vs Biological.

Zerg.name: Roach Warren
Zerg.note: Roach/Ravager; roach upgrades.

Zerg.name: Baneling Nest
Zerg.note: Banelings; Centrifugal Hooks.

Zerg.name: Hydralisk Den
Zerg.note: Hydralisks; range/speed upgrades.

Zerg.name: Lurker Den
Zerg.note: Lurkers; Adaptive Talons, Seismic Spines.

Zerg.name: Infestation Pit
Zerg.note: Infestor, Swarm Host; enables Hive.

Zerg.name: Nydus Network
Zerg.note: Deploys Nydus Worms for map-wide transport.

Zerg.name: Spire
Zerg.note: 5.0.15 cost 200->150, time 71->66. Mutalisk, Corruptor; flyer upgrades.

Zerg.name: Greater Spire
Zerg.note: +cost on top of Spire. Brood Lord; flyer L3 upgrades.

Zerg.name: Ultralisk Cavern
Zerg.note: Ultralisks; Chitinous Plating, Anabolic Synthesis.

Terran.name: Stimpack
Terran.effect: Marine/Marauder spend HP for temporary attack & move speed.

Terran.name: Drilling Claws
Terran.effect: Widow Mine faster burrow; cloak while reloading.

Terran.name: Interference Matrix
Terran.effect: Raven disables a mech/psionic unit ~11s (now needs research).

Protoss.name: Warp Gate (research)
Protoss.effect: Enables Gateway->Warp Gate (transform 25/25 each, 5.0.16); ~40% faster Gateway production.

Zerg.name: Microbial Shroud
Zerg.effect: Infestor anti-air damage-reduction cloud (5.0.16: no longer needs upgrade; kept for reference).

benchmarks.note: Community/empirical reference landmarks in the FASTER in-game clock (mm:ss). Use to sanity-check computed timings. Travel-dependent events (proxy hits) vary with map/spawn distance. These are approximate, not derived from the tables above.
